name: modal
description: A centred overlay dialog, headless CONTROLLED / UNCONTROLLED (architecture contract overlay doctrine). UNCONTROLLED (default): the trigger is a real <button command="show-modal" commandfor> (browser invoker command API); the browser opens the <dialog> as a native modal (top-layer, ::backdrop, focus-trap, Escape) with zero JS; every close path is a <form method="dialog"> submit. CONTROLLED (open param set by the caller's @Wire field): pass open + closeAction; the dialog renders with the HTML `open` attribute when open=true; a positioned scrim covers the viewport; data-lievit-focus-trap activates the shared focus-trap enhancer (Tab cycling, scroll-lock, Esc fires closeAction); when open=false the dialog is hidden (absent from a11y tree). Closable controls the X button + scrim-click + Esc together. WAI-ARIA APG Dialog (Modal): role=dialog, aria-modal=true, aria-labelledby/aria-describedby, icon-only close button with aria-label. Content slots: content (owned body, never a slot), heading + description (labelled dialog), footer (actions). Sizes sm/md/lg/xl map to panel max-width. No Java component, no @Wire field.
docs: Copies the modal partial to your JTE root at lievit/modal.jte, reached as @template.lievit.modal(...). UNCONTROLLED (default): pass a `trigger` content slot and a unique `id` -- the browser opens and closes the dialog natively with zero JS (command="show-modal"). CONTROLLED: pass `open` (your @Wire boolean) and `closeAction` (the wire action name) to let the server own the open-state; the focus-trap.enhancer.ts handles the focus cycle + scroll-lock + Esc. Both modes support: `heading` + `description` (labelled dialog), `footer` (action buttons), `size` (sm|md|lg|xl), `closable` (X + Esc + scrim, default true). Install the focus-trap enhancer when using controlled mode: import installFocusTrap from your lievit runtime features and call it with the runtime instance.
